decision: candidate-only adversarial search; any apparent recurrence is a finite numerical lead requiring independent reproduction, not a Navier--Stokes theorem.

objective.endpoint: maximize min_m P_L((m+1)T) / P_L(mT), where P_L follows the dominant shell and T = c * 2^(-2j) / nu
objective.static_prefilter: maximize initial nonlinear target-shell inflow only to seed an endpoint search
objective.translation_quotient: checkpoint correlation is maximised over discrete torus translations; lattice rotations are not quotiented
objective.warning: this version samples phase candidates and evolves only a staged prefiltered subset; it is not global endpoint optimization.  A selected static helicity case is an envelope seed, not a joint leaf-choice or mixed-polarization search.

phase_audit.warning: A graph cycle alone does not determine an NS phase holonomy; any next holonomy audit must choose a channel weighting and a phase-preference branch.

topology.constraints: reality symmetric, divergence free helical modes, componentwise 2/3 dealiased N64 support, fixed role amplitudes and declared global normalization

helicity_factorisation_geometry.elimination_note: For each backbone assignment retain local Pareto alternatives over coupling magnitude, channel phase, desired-leg transfer sign, and helicity metadata; do not eliminate a leaf solely by magnitude.
helicity_factorisation_geometry.global_helicity_flip: not quotiented until endpoint-objective invariance is verified
helicity_factorisation_geometry.pure_mode_helicity_model: one sign per represented mode; reality fixes the negative partner

helicity_static_factor_audit.warning: The ranking is an upper-envelope screen, not a phase optimization or an endpoint prediction. Local choices retain coupling phase because maximal magnitude alone can destroy network compatibility.

helical_coupling_holonomy.reason: requires exact complex helical-channel coefficients, not only triad/mode topology

role_mass_allocation.construction: role weights use the packet ledger's dyadic shell factor; final shares are recomputed from the finite carrier

selected_helicity_seed.warning: private leaves use each factor's largest-envelope alternative only; this is a reproducible seed, not a joint phase/share optimisation

signed_phase_constraint_geometry.coordinate_convention: reality-reduced phases: canonical labelled channel row is phi(p)+phi(q)+phi(r) after phi(-p)=-phi(p)
signed_phase_constraint_geometry.static_holonomy_conclusion: no topology-forced phase holonomy: every constraint has a private phase variable, so the rows are independent
signed_phase_constraint_geometry.warning: This does not rule out dynamical relocking. It only rules out a static phase-frustration theorem from this graph alone.

topology.translation_phase_gauge: fixed phases of [(8, 0, 0), (0, 8, 0), (0, 0, 8)] remove the three translation gauges for the cyclic graph
